title: Authorization, Tenancy, Object, Function, and Property Policy · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs authorization, tenancy, object, function, and property policy from bounded code and request evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the deny-by-default resource authorization decision. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deny-by-default resource authorization decision after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Authorize a validated subject, resource, action, context, and requested properties at the resource boundary before effects.
Load only enough identity or policy data to enforce ownership and object access without trusting a user-selected identifier.
Derive tenant from the principal, carry it through repository interfaces, constrain every query and cache key, and test cross-tenant changed cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Denial Disclosure Audit
steps.instruction:
review case — A review team handles case 1397 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce tenant, object action, and property policy as one deny-by-default decision; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 behavior consistently and preserve bounded decision evidence without leaking sensitive resource existence or policy internals.

Inspect the Denial Disclosure Audit trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Denial Disclosure Audit.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 behavior consistently and preserve bounded decision evidence without leaking sensitive resource existence or policy internals. For Node and Express server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Denial Disclosure Audit, keep Denial Disclosure Audit connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: For Denial Disclosure Audit, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 behavior consistently and preserve bounded decision evidence without leaking sensitive resource existence or policy internals.

For Node and Express server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Denial Disclosure Audit, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Every authorization denial should explain the complete policy failure to the caller.

lines.label: risk

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 behavior consistently and preserve bounded decision evidence without leaking sensitive resource existence or policy internals. Artifact under review: deny-by-default resource authorization decision.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Denial Disclosure Audit, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Denial Disclosure Audit record says deny-by-default resource authorization decision looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Denial Disclosure Audit.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient Denial Disclosure Audit happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Denial Disclosure Audit for deny-by-default resource authorization decision.
